perturbation.original_text: 3. If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any Co nfidential Material, the Stockholder will provide the Company with prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such Confidential Material as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any information covered by this agreement, the Stockholder will provide the Company with prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such information covered by this agreement as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any information covered by this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Confidential Material" with "information covered by this agreement" in the first part of Section 3, but failing to do so consistently throughout the same section, creates an in-text contradiction. The phrase "Confidential Material" still appears in Section 3. This introduces ambiguity as to which standard applies for disclosure obligations. The Section itself becomes internally inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. If the Stockholder is required (by interrogatory, subpoena, civil investigatory demand, or any similar process relating to any legal proceeding, investigation, hearing, or otherwise) to disclose in any manner any information covered by this agreement, the Stockholder will provide the Company with prompt notice (i.e., within 2 business days of receipt of such interrogatory, subpoena, dema nd, or any similar process), so that the Company may seek a protective order or other appropriat e remedy and/or waive compliance with this Agreement in the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Company waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, the Stockholder may furnish only such information covered by this agreement as they are advised is legally required and must exercise their best effo rts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Confidential Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Confidential Material" with "information covered by this agreement" in the initial part of Section 3 but fails to consistently apply this change throughout the section. Specifically, the phrase "Confidential Material" remains in the last part of the sentence. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it becomes unclear whether disclosure obligations apply to 'Confidential Material' or 'information covered by this agreement', or both. The contradicted text which is the same as the changed text, demonstrates the contradiction as it references both terms, creating internal inconsistency within Section 3 itself. This aligns with the Omissions - In Text Contradiction perturbation type, because essential language was altered without consistency creating a contradiction with the unaltered parts of the section.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Each Party who receives Confidential Material pursuant to this Agreement agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the De laware Court of Chancery in any action to enforce any provision of, or based on any ri ght or claim arising out of, this Agreement 21499249 including any action to determine any dispute as to the confidentiality of any information or document produced by the Company. Each Party hereby expressly waives any and all objections such Party may ha ve to personal jurisdiction or to venue, including without limitation th e inconvenience of such forum, in the Court of Chancery.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Each Party who receives information pursuant to this Agreement agrees to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the De laware Court of Chancery in any action to enforce any provision of, or based on any ri ght or claim arising out of, this Agreement 21499249 including any action to determine any dispute as to the confidentiality of any information or document produced by the Company. Each Party hereby expressly waives any and all objections such Party may ha ve to personal jurisdiction or to venue, including without limitation th e inconvenience of such forum, in the Court of Chancery.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Material" to "information" in Section 4 makes the scope of jurisdiction less clear. While the section refers to resolving disputes concerning the confidentiality of 'any information', the agreement itself previously defined a specific set of information as 'Confidential Material'. This shift creates uncertainty about whether the court's jurisdiction is limited to only information that would have fallen under the original definition of “Confidential Material” or includes all information, which contradicts Section 1 that specifies Confidential Material. The full text of Section 4 is impacted by this changed definition. This causes contradiction with Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Stockholder agrees that the Confidential Material is being produced to them only for purposes reasonably related to Stockholder’ s interests as a stockholder of the Company and that Stockholder shall not utilize Conf idential Material, any other information produced by the Company or anything derive d from Confidential Material or derived from any other information produced by the Company for any other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Material,' which is a defined term in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Material' with 'information' in Section 4. This creates a contradiction with Section 1 because Section 1 restricts the use of Confidential Material for purposes related to the stockholder's interest, while the changed Section 4 suggests a broader jurisdiction related to 'any information', potentially conflicting with the specific limitations of 'Confidential Material' outlined in Section 1. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, where changing essential language creates a contradiction with other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Each Party agrees that the Company does no t have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Company will suffer irreparable harm and will be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to any other remedies to which the Company ma y be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Each Party agrees that the Company does no t have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder regarding confidential information. The Parties further agree that, in the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Stockholder, the Company will suffer irreparable harm and will be entitled to specific performance and/or injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for any breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to any other remedies to which the Company ma y be entitled at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "regarding confidential information" to the first sentence of Section 5 creates ambiguity because 'Confidential Information' isn't defined. This suggests injunctive relief is only available for breaches relating to confidential information. However, the following sentences state that remedies are available for *any* breach. This is a contradiction in the scope of available legal remedies. This causes contradiction with Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Stockholder will not disclose, publish, or communicate information obtained under this agreement to any person or entity in any form (written, oral , electronic or through any other medium), either directly or indirectly, except as pr ovided in this Confidentiality Agreement to end; provided that in no event shall stockh older, directly or indirectly, publically disclose, publish or communicate names, addr esses, holdings of any other stockholder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "regarding confidential information" to the first sentence of Section 5, limiting the scope of breaches that warrant injunctive relief. This contradicts the subsequent sentences in Section 5, which state that remedies are available for *any* breach, as well as Section 2, which broadly prohibits the disclosure of information and does not limit remedies to only confidential information breaches. This creates a direct conflict regarding the scope of available remedies and makes the contract unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 10. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all pr ior agreements and understandings, written and oral, between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with regard to the disclosure of confidential information, and supersedes all pr ior agreements and understandings, written and oral, between the Parties with regard to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the scope of the entire agreement clause in Section 10 to "disclosure of confidential information," while also stating it supersedes all prior agreements regarding the *subject matter* hereof, a contradiction is created. If the subject matter is disclosure of confidential information, that may be narrower than the other sections. The agreement now implies that other aspects of the Stockholder's relationship with the company are *not* fully covered by this agreement, potentially contradicting other sections that assume complete coverage. Section 1 now creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Stockholder agrees that the Confidential Material is being produced to them only for purposes reasonably related to Stockholder’ s interests as a stockholder of the Company and that Stockholder shall not utilize information obtained under this agreement, any other information produced by the Company or anything derive d from information obtained under this agreement or derived from any other information produced by the Company for any other purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text, Section 10, states that the agreement constitutes the *entire* agreement between the parties, superseding prior agreements. The changed text narrows this scope, limiting it to 'disclosure of confidential information'. This creates a contradiction with Section 1, which concerns the stockholder's use of Confidential Material beyond just disclosure. By limiting Section 10's scope, it implies that aspects of the stockholder's interests as a stockholder (as described in Section 1) are *not* fully covered by this agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
